summary: The test suite validates basic structural requirements (workflow syntax, script syntax, fixture existence, actionlint compliance) and exercises the main happy path through an act integration test with hard-coded assertions on specific fixture data (17 tests, 10 passed, 5 failed, 2 skipped). However, it lacks unit-level tests for individual parser functions and has no coverage of error scenarios (malformed XML/JSON, missing files, empty inputs, write failures) or edge cases (all passed, all failed, all skipped, no flaky tests, special characters in names). The test is brittle—assertions on fixture content ("TOTAL_TESTS=17") are tightly coupled to undocumented fixture structure, making the suite fragile if fixtures change. No tests verify the actual markdown output content or error messages. The suite would catch syntax regressions and basic workflow misconfiguration, but would miss functional bugs in parsing logic, edge case handling, and error reporting.
